title: Housing Discrimination Assistance * Agricultural Laborers - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Housing Discrimination Assistance * Agricultural Laborers 2 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Agricultural Laborers No Filter Agricultural Laborers Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON FOREST GROVE FARMWORKER OFFICE FARMWORKER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help to migrant and seasonal farmworkers with employment, housing, unemployment, and tax issues. Most employment and housing issues are related to payment of wages, unauthorized pay deductions, poor living conditions in labor camps, retaliation for asserting legal rights, poor health and safety conditions on the job, pesticide problems, and discrimination. Also gives referrals to other kinds of legal programs. Eligibility: Agricultural (including seasonal) workers who have experienced civil legal problems in the State of Oregon Hours: By appointment only (503) 214-1384 Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON SALEM FARMWORKER OFFICE FARMWORKER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help to migrant and seasonal farmworkers with employment, housing, unemployment, and tax issues. Most employment and housing issues are related to payment of wages, unauthorized pay deductions, poor living conditions in labor camps, retaliation for asserting legal rights, poor health and safety conditions on the job, pesticide problems, and discrimination. Also gives referrals to other kinds of legal programs. Eligibility: Agricultural (including seasonal) workers who have experienced civil legal problems in the State of Oregon Hours: By appointment only (503) 981-5291 Visit Website More Details No Mapping Information Available
